title: anacamptis morio subsp. albanica
intro: Anacamptis morio subsp. albanica, also known as the Albanian pyramidal orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Anacamptis morio subsp. albanica are typically dried and used as a decoction or tincture, although further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and applications.
